allowedConsumers.core/workforce: Provision and manage workforce actors through authenticated internal APIs; never stores identity passwords or session tokens.
allowedConsumers.services/dsh: Consumes resolved actor subject, role, permission, surface, and tenant context; never owns credentials or sessions.
allowedConsumers.services/wlt: Consumes trusted actor references only; no authentication or session ownership.
allowedConsumers.apps/*: Consume the sovereign TypeScript client and session store only.

forbiddenOwnership: DSH must not create identity sessions.
WLT must not authenticate actors or persist identity tokens.
Workforce must not persist a second phone truth or password hash.
UI surfaces must not synthesize roles, permissions, tenantId, sessionId, or phoneE164.
No service may expose an impersonation/support session without a separately approved contract, migration, audit model, and security review.
crossServiceRules: Internal actor administration requires X-Service-Caller=workforce and a constant-time verified service bearer token.
Identity deletion emits outbox work; downstream deletion/anonymization is asynchronous and idempotent.
Financial state and wallet authorization remain exclusively owned by WLT and are outside JRN-002.
